metadata.title: Demo risk scenario mapping
metadata.notes: Maps common control failures to leadership-readable risk scenarios.

scenarios.title: Unauthorized privileged access
scenarios.summary: Unauthorized access risk increases when account lifecycle controls and strong authentication are not operating effectively.
scenarios.response: Disable inactive privileged accounts, enforce MFA for administrative access, and review exception handling.
scenarios.statement_template: Unauthorized access risk increases because {controls} control failures indicate weaknesses in account lifecycle and authentication enforcement. This creates a plausible path for misuse of orphaned or weakly protected privileged accounts.

control_mappings.rationale: Account management failures can leave orphaned or excessive access active.
Weak authentication increases the probability of account takeover.
Least privilege failures increase blast radius after compromise.

scenarios.title: Delayed detection of suspicious activity
scenarios.summary: Detection and response risk increases when audit review practices are inconsistent or incomplete.
scenarios.response: Define log review cadence, automate alert routing, and track review evidence for high-value systems.
scenarios.statement_template: Delayed detection risk increases because {controls} control failures reduce confidence that suspicious activity will be reviewed and escalated in time to limit business impact.

control_mappings.rationale: Audit review gaps delay identification of suspicious activity.
Incident monitoring issues weaken escalation and coordination.

scenarios.title: External attack surface exposure
scenarios.summary: Exposure risk increases when boundary protections and configuration baselines are not consistently enforced.
scenarios.response: Restrict inbound administrative access, review security group exceptions, and remediate baseline drift.
scenarios.statement_template: External attack surface risk increases because {controls} control failures suggest exposed network paths and configuration drift that could be used to reach production systems.

control_mappings.rationale: Boundary protection failures can expose services or administrative access paths.
Configuration drift increases exploitability and operational inconsistency.
Weak vulnerability monitoring increases exposure duration.
